description: Use this API to return an array of real values for subsystem FACTS device buses. This API routine was first introduced in release 30.3.0. ierr, rarray = afactsbusreal(sid, ties, flag, fcttyp, entry, string)
python_syntax: ierr, rarray = afactsbusreal(sid, ties, flag, fcttyp, entry, string)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: Defines the bus subsystem to be used. (input; -1 by default).

parameters.name: ties
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem buses connected to facts devices with a series element to include (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: flag
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem facts devices to include. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: fcttyp
parameters.description: A flag indicating which types of facts devices to include. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: entry
parameters.description: A flag indicating organization. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: An array of NSTR elements specifying NSTR of the following strings indicating the FACTS device quantities desired. (input; no default allowed).

return_values.name: rarray
return_values.description: An array of NSTR*DIM elements specifying Is an array of at least NSTR columns, each of which contains DIM elements. (output).

sid.description: to instruct the API to assume a subsystem containing all buses in the working case.
valid bus subsystem identifier. Valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. Subsystem SID must have been previously defined.

ties.description: for each end of interior subsystem FACTS devices only.
for the subsystem bus end of tie FACTS devices only.
for the non-subsystem bus end of tie FACTS devices only.
for each end of tie FACTS devices only.
for each end of interior subsystem FACTS devices and the subsystem bus end of tie FACTS devices.
for each end of interior subsystem FACTS devices and tie FACTS devices.

flag.description: for both in-service and out-of-service FACTS devices.

fcttyp.description: for only FACTS devices without a series element.
for only FACTS devices with a series element.
for both types of FACTS devices.

string.description: Series voltage magnitude in pu.
Current flow into series element at the from bus end in pu.
Current flow into shunt element in pu (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus).
Current flow into series element at the from bus end in amps.
Current flow into shunt element in amps (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus).
Percent series current loading at the from bus end.
Percent shunt current loading (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus).
Percent bridge active power loading (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus).
Active power flow into the series element at the from bus end, in MW.
Reactive power flow into the series element at the from bus end, in Mvar.
Bridge active power exchange (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus), in MW.
Reactive power flow into the shunt element (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus), in Mvar.
Power flow into the series element at the from bus end, in MVA.
Power flow into the shunt element (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus), in MVA.
Reactive power losses, in Mvar.
Active power flow into the series element at the from bus end (units determined by power output options).
Reactive power flow into the series element at the from bus end (units determined by power output options).
Bridge active power exchange (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus) (units determined by power output options).
Reactive power flow into the shunt element (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus) (units determined by power output options).
Power flow into the series element at the from bus end (units determined by power output options).
Power flow into the shunt element (0.0 if the from bus is the terminal end bus) (units determined by power output options).
Reactive power losses (units determined by power output options).

error_codes.description: DIM, and hence the size of RARRAY, is not large enough.
